---
title: My visual data story
output: html_document
---

<script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.7.1.min.js" integrity="sha256-/JqT3SQfawRcv/BIHPThkBvs0OEvtFFmqPF/lYI/Cxo=" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
```

```{js}
$(function() {
  $(".level2").css('visibility', 'hidden');
  $(".level2").first().css('visibility', 'visible');
  $(".container-fluid").height($(".container-fluid").height() + 300);
  $(window).on('scroll', function() {
    $('h2').each(function() {
      var h2Top = $(this).offset().top - $(window).scrollTop();
      var windowHeight = $(window).height();
      if (h2Top >= 0 && h2Top <= windowHeight / 2) {
        $(this).parent('div').css('visibility', 'visible');
      } else if (h2Top > windowHeight / 2) {
        $(this).parent('div').css('visibility', 'hidden');
      }
    });
  });
})
```

```{css}
.figcaption {display: none}
```
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)

# Read the CSV data from my Project 2 Google Form
data_url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRiqEG6I5hXeg7G_AUod36rHLdspdr5f_EtcdSmT8Z6ztN7D6iJzCp4HL_4Q7Ety9g-i9l6UKqIW8rc/pub?output=csv"

logged_data <- read_csv(data_url)

# Rename the columns to make them easier to use in R
colnames(logged_data) <- c("Timestamp", "activity", "location", "time_period")

# Clean and prepare the data
logged_data <- logged_data %>%
  mutate(
    Timestamp = parse_date_time(
      Timestamp,
      orders = c("dmy HMS", "mdy HMS", "ymd HMS", "dmy IMp", "mdy IMp")
    ),
    activity = as.factor(activity),
    location = as.factor(location),
    time_period = factor(
      time_period,
      levels = c(
        "Morning (6:00 am–11:59 am)",
        "Afternoon (12:00 pm–5:59 pm)",
        "Evening (6:00 pm–9:59 pm)",
        "Late night (10:00 pm–1:59 am)"
      )
    ),
    hour = hour(Timestamp),
    strength_training = if_else(
      str_detect(activity, "Strength Training"),
      "Yes",
      "No"
    )
  )

# Plot 1: Activity types
activity_plot <- ggplot(logged_data, aes(x = activity)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed Activity Types",
    x = "Activity Type",
    y = "Number of Observations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

activity_plot


# Plot 2: Observations by location
location_summary <- logged_data %>%
  group_by(location) %>%
  summarise(
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

location_plot <- ggplot(location_summary, 
                        aes(x = reorder(location, observations), y = observations)) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Observations by Location",
    x = "Location",
    y = "Number of Observations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip()

location_plot


# Plot 3: Activity types across time periods
activity_time_summary <- logged_data %>%
  count(activity, time_period)

activity_time_plot <- ggplot(activity_time_summary,
                             aes(x = time_period, y = activity, size = n)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Activity Types Across Different Time Periods",
    x = "Time Period",
    y = "Activity Type",
    size = "Number of Observations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

activity_time_plot

# Plot 4: Observations by hour of the 

hour_summary <- logged_data %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarise(
    observations = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(hour)

hour_plot <- ggplot(hour_summary, aes(x = hour, y = observations)) +
  geom_line(group = 1, colour = "purple") +
  geom_point(colour = "purple", size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Observations by Hour of the Day",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Number of Observations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

hour_plot
